title: Swetharanyeswarar Temple
tagline: Swetharanyeswarar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Swetharanyeswarar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Thiruvenkadu, a village in Mayiladuthurai district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu.
overview.description: Swetharanyeswarar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Thiruvenkadu, a village in Mayiladuthurai district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshiped as Swetharanyeswarar, and is represented by the lingam. His consort Parvati is depicted as Brahmavidyambigai. The presiding deity is revered in the 7th-century-CE Tamil Saiva canonical work, the Tevaram, written by Tamil saint poets known as the Nayanars and classified as Paadal Petra Sthalam.

topPlaces.name: Thiruvenkadu
topPlaces.description: Thiruvenkadu (also spelled Thiruvengadu) is a village in Sirkazhi taluk of Mayiladuthurai district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is located 12 kilometres (7.5 mi) from the taluk headquarter, Sirkazhi.

topPlaces.name: Thiruppaarththanpalli
topPlaces.description: The Taamaraiyaal Kelvan Perumal Temple or Thiruppaarththanpalli is located close to Thirunangur, a small village, 8 km east of Sirkali en route to Thiruvenkadu and is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Taamariyaal Kelvan and his consort Lakshmi as Shegamalavalli.

topPlaces.name: Manigramam, Tamil Nadu
topPlaces.description: Manigramam is a village in Mayiladuthurai district in the southern Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is located 25 km from Mayiladuthurai (Mayuram), 18 km from Sirkali, 5 km from Poompuhar, 2 km from Thiruvengadu.

topPlaces.name: Thirumanikkoodam
topPlaces.description: Thirumanikkoodam or Varadaraja Perumal Temple is located in Thirunangur, a village in the outskirts of Sirkaḻi in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu.

topPlaces.name: Thiruchsemponsey
topPlaces.description: Thiruchsemponsey or Perarulaalan Perumal Temple is dedicated to Hindu god Vishnu located in Thirunangur, a village in the outskirts of Sirkazhi in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries AD. It is one of the 108 Divya Desam dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Peralulalan and his consort Lakshmi as Allimalar Nachiyar.

topPlaces.name: Annan Koil
topPlaces.description: Annan Koil is a village near Sirkazhi in Mayiladuthurai district of Tamil Nadu, (South India). The village is irrigated by the river Kaveri which flows into the Bay Of Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Thiruvanpurushothamam
topPlaces.description: The Purushotama Perumal Temple is located in Thirunangur, a village in the outskirts of Sirkazhi in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, and is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Purushottaman and his consort Lakshmi as Purushottama Nayagi.

topPlaces.name: Thirumanimadam
topPlaces.description: Thirumanimadam or Narayanan Perumal Temple (also called Badrinarayana Perumal Temple) is dedicated to Hindu god Vishnu located in Thirunangur, a village in the outskirts of Sirkazhi in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Narayanan and his consort Lakshmi as Pundarikavalli.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Swetharanyeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Swetharanyeswarar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Swetharanyeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (128 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (129 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Swetharanyeswarar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Swetharanyeswarar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Swetharanyeswarar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Swetharanyeswarar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Swetharanyeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thiruvenkadu, Thiruppaarththanpalli, Manigramam, Tamil Nadu, Thirumanikkoodam, Thiruchsemponsey.
